text: Three robotic trochors were positioned.

and connected to robotic arms from back to front as follows robotic 30 degree camera arm robotic arm 2 and robotic arm 1 the most anterior part of the access was used as the assistant access and no CO2 was used as you can see here retraction of the lung is made by the assistant in our experience assistant role was quite important and experience in minimally invasive surgery was necessary at the operating table
Here you can see an external view of the operating field with the three robotic arms and the assistant at the most interior part of the axis.
Highly lymph nodes are found and resected to better expose the pulmonary artery.
For a better view, the first venous branch is isolated and stapled.
During the operation, instruments used were cadier forceps, long bipolar grasper and monopolar hook.
Manual stapler was inserted by the assistant.
Here the first apicoposterior branch of the pulmonary artery is isolated and stapled.
A small bleeding occurred and was controlled with clips.
The second apicoposterior arterial branch is ligated with clips and cut.
Here the fissure is approached and an additional apicoposteroid branch is found and stapled.
Completion of the arterial elements is made by isolating and stapling the remaining anterior branch.
Subsequently, the remaining second venous branch is isolated and stapled.
As said before, in this part the assistant role was quite relevant.
Lastly, upper division bronchus is isolated and stapled after reinflation of the lung.
After arterial stapling, indesonating green was injected.
An infrared view was used to identify intersubmental planes.
After marking, lang was stapled.
An intercostal nerve lock was used at the end of the operation.
Final pathology will reveal a stage PT1B and 0 adenocarcinoma.
Harvested lymph node stations were number 10, 11, 5 and 7.
